title: lithocarpus dealbatus subsp. eudealbatus
intro: Lithocarpus dealbatus subsp. eudealbatus, also known as the Taiwan red oak or Taiwan evergreen oak, is a subspecies of evergreen tree that belongs to the beech family (Fagaceae). The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Lithocarpus dealbatus subsp. eudealbatus include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory diseases such as bronchitis and asthma. Lithocarpus dealbatus subsp. eudealbatus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
